tweets.text: The United States is working with our partners in Mexico and Guatemala to address the root causes of irregular migration. https://t.co/KFDRF7ajN0

Around the world, more people are on the move than at any time in recorded history. When it comes to irregular migration, we have to meet this together – not only among Mexico, Guatemala, and the U.S., but all the countries in the region. https://t.co/zvnYQhCjAN

.@SecBlinken met with Mexican and Guatemalan officials today to address irregular migration in the hemisphere: "We have a shared commitment to safe, orderly, humane migration." https://t.co/JvTKBT6EaD

The rainforest is one of the world's greatest natural resources when it comes to actually effectively dealing with climate change carbon emissions. That’s why the U.S. partners with Brazil and other countries to manage and preserve rainforests. https://t.co/l86qQcn1At

The impacts of climate change are already here and air quality in countries around the world will worsen. No country can solve the climate crisis on its own, which is why we must work together. https://t.co/E7qYIlOpR0
When we work together with other nations, we can effectively meet the moment and address the needs of the people that we represent. https://t.co/j1eUvz1RWr

It is Afghanistan’s loss if women and girls are not allowed to reach their full potential. 

The Taliban’s restrictions are suffocating Afghanistan's potential. https://t.co/fShjUp9Etk

The U.S. condemns Russia’s unjust sentencing of Oleg Orlov, the renowned Russian human rights leader and cochair of the 2022 Nobel Prize-winning organization Memorial. Together with our allies and partners, we strongly condemn the Kremlin’s escalating domestic repression. https://t.co/V8xK3Uglhf

.@WelcomeCorps allows Americans to do what we do best: be guides and friends to our neighbors and communities — to the benefit of not just refugee families, but all our families. Learn more: https://t.co/Lmp5EhigVP

.@SecBlinken delivers remarks at the Alliance for Afghan Women’s Economic Resilience Summit. Co-hosted with @BU_Tweets, the Summit will engage stakeholders on how to work on shared objectives and support Afghanistan. https://t.co/RTquq8VlOx  https://t.co/ei11WVFCpT
.@SecBlinken explains why we are not giving up on Ukraine in the latest edition of From The Secretary’s Desk. https://t.co/adOYAbE8n2 https://t.co/dOcElWXHvG
Take 🎶 as we look back on Black artists who have left their mark — not only on the music world, but on diplomacy. https://t.co/BLUOancWYR https://t.co/ndqTztxmES

Ambassador Terence Todman paved the way for Black Americans in the Foreign Service when he joined the Foreign Service in the 1950s. While facing resistance, racism, and segregation during his career, he moved the Department to take action and provide dignity for Black employees. https://t.co/Xe0o7poNxT

This week @SecBlinken traveled to Brazil and Argentina, including a stop in Rio de Janeiro for the @g20org. This weekend also marks two years since Putin began his full-scale invasion of Ukraine. The U.S. stands with Ukraine. https://t.co/bm0vyNWR4J

A decade ago, Ukraine’s Revolution of Dignity led to a government by and for Ukrainians, standing up to Russia in support of democracy. Ukrainians continue to stand united against Russia’s aggression two years after Putin’s full-scale invasion. https://t.co/q2rtlQHm8p
Two years later, the world remains united with Ukraine. https://t.co/Okj6wsSKDj
Ukraine stands for values we all share: Democracy, Self-determination, Freedom. Two years after Putin’s full-scale invasion, we stand united with Ukraine. https://t.co/Tb2SYKSFw7

Ukraine’s victories on and off the battlefield illustrate the country’s resilience and commitment to its sovereignty from Russia. Today and every day, the United States stands #UnitedWithUkraine. https://t.co/Yl7XlRW1BA

.@StateDeptSpox breaks down the U.S. sanctions to hold Russia accountable — including the sanctioning of three individuals connected to the death of Aleksey Navalny.

The United States will continue to stand with Ukraine. https://t.co/V04fsqm3JB

Today, the U.S., in coordination with many allies and partners, is taking additional measures to respond to Russia's full-scale war and intensifying repression at home by sanctioning & imposing export control restrictions on more than 500 individuals and entities. - @SecBlinken https://t.co/nkZMZYWKjx

.@SecBlinken ’s visit to Argentina highlights our strong relationship after more than 200 years of diplomatic ties. Together, we are partnering to advance human rights, democracy, prosperity, and address the climate crisis. https://t.co/K3RC4wysUg
.@SecBlinken participates in a joint press availability with Argentine Foreign Minister @DianaMondino in Buenos Aires, Argentina. https://t.co/KbdhPA8Dwj https://t.co/KPn1hTCGh4
.@SecBlinken holds a press availability at the @g20org in Rio de Janeiro, Brazil. https://t.co/mrzmMtKTBN https://t.co/q7B3U12Qjc

They’ll discuss everything from food insecurity and climate change to global health and workers’ rights. The U.S. is working with our partners to build a more prosperous, sustainable, and inclusive global economy that delivers good jobs, food, energy, and health security for all. https://t.co/GDjdsCFtaR

The @g20org brings countries together to work on international economic cooperation and other global issues. This year, Brazil holds the rotating presidency and foreign ministers are meeting in Rio this week ahead of the Leaders Summit in November. https://t.co/keRc3LGyFx
History shows us: If we allow an aggressor like Putin to take land with impunity, they keep going. The other would-be aggressors then become emboldened. - @VP Harris https://t.co/Tr7YMD86xf

.@SecBlinken is in Brazil this week. He’ll meet with President Lula in the capital of Brasilia before heading to Rio de Janeiro to engage with other foreign ministers at the #G20Brazil. https://t.co/GRyIESXUJp

International Mother Language Day is a time to celebrate linguistic diversity and support the protection of all languages. Here at the State Department, we spoke with some of our colleagues about their favorite saying in their mother tongue. https://t.co/XT1354xacW
Support for Ukraine is not just from the United States, it’s from country after country in Europe and well beyond. https://t.co/JvO8kkFCRQ

The brutality of Putin’s regime is increasingly evident both at home and abroad. The weakness and rot at the heart of the system Putin built was confirmed not only by Aleksey Navalny’s death, but also by Russia detaining close to 400 people just for mourning his passing. https://t.co/XGRgBqwiOQ

The people of Ukraine have demonstrated remarkable courage and resilience. The United States will continue to stand with them until Ukraine secures a just and durable peace. - @SecBlinken https://t.co/R1SpwakTHI
And now that you’ve taken our Presidents’ Day quiz, take a look back at how photography has shaped public perception of U.S. presidents during their lifetimes and long after. https://t.co/lJRzn9Fzhq

Can you identify the U.S. President in these famous photos in U.S. history? From the earliest forms of photography to the digital age, photos have been a central part of a modern president’s outreach. https://t.co/Ap2Q2UNJFm

Today is Presidents Day in the United States, a day to honor all American presidents, past and present, and the legacy and impact of the U.S. presidency on our country and the world. https://t.co/PCcqpWLE7B

This week @SecBlinken convened U.S. government leaders on our efforts to #EndHumanTrafficking. He also traveled to Albania — a key partner for stability in the Western Balkans — before joining @VP at the #MunichSecurityConference. https://t.co/3WgZnGhbON
.@NATO has deterred aggression against its members to the benefit of the security of the American people. For the past 75 years, NATO members have maintained this solemn pact. https://t.co/AqYdKgALAF

Last Sunday’s Super Bowl between the Kansas City Chiefs and San Francisco 49ers broke records with over 123 million viewers tuning in for #SBLVIII across the U.S. Many football fans also watched the game overseas, with the State Department hosting some epic watch parties.
America must stand strong for democracy. We must stand in defense of international rules and norms, and we must stand with our allies. - @VP https://t.co/4Nq9Gl4j2c

.@SecBlinken participates in a public forum with Indian External Affairs Minister Jaishankar and German Foreign Minister Baerbock on seizing shared opportunities from the @MunSecConf in Germany. https://t.co/dfWmnXahvb

We are committed to pursue global engagement, to uphold international rules and norms, to defend democratic values at home and abroad, and to work with our allies and partners in pursuit of shared goals.  -@VP at #MSC2024 https://t.co/V9ZFVzYgDw
Make no mistake, Putin’s war has already been an utter failure for Russia. -@VP at #MSC2024 https://t.co/aqYnOcqKpQ
.@VP and @SecBlinken are in Munich this week for the @MunSecConf, joining our allies and partners to discuss the world’s most pressing issues.

Following reports of Aleksey Navalny’s death in Russian prison, @SecBlinken: If these reports bear out to be true, his death in a Russian prison — and the fixation and fear of one man — only underscores the weakness and rot at the heart of the system that Putin has built. https://t.co/BrlJY4wWRE
.@VP Harris delivers remarks at the #MunichSecurityConference: https://t.co/rraenp4nPU

Houthi attacks on ships in the Red Sea & nearby Gulf of Aden have raised costs for businesses. The longer route can add $1 million in fuel costs for a ship bound for northern Europe around the Cape of Good Hope — costs generally passed along to consumers. https://t.co/AG7VBb66Yh https://t.co/MSn5NwmNAd

.@SecBlinken is in the Albanian capital of Tirana today. The U.S. is partnering with Albania on many issues, including boosting its cyber defenses, standing together to support Ukraine, and working to resettle thousands of people from Afghanistan. https://t.co/9u5mMJpSVY https://t.co/kKfhlOr913

.@SecBlinken participates in signing ceremonies for U.S.-Albania agreements that will strengthen educational exchanges and counter disinformation, followed by a joint press availability with Albanian Prime Minister @EdiRamaAL. https://t.co/moucodX4ON https://t.co/zOe9tVRC3C

The Houthis’ attacks on ships in the Red Sea are hurting people around the world, especially the poorest and most vulnerable. Aggressive Houthi acts mean rerouting thousands of ships, causing delays and increasing the cost of food, fuel, medicine, and humanitarian assistance. https://t.co/2DzHilVN35

.@POTUS : The major military operation in Rafah should not proceed without a credible plan — a credible plan for ensuring the safety and support of more than one million people sheltering there. https://t.co/SRcgPeyEZv

This week in Paris, the United States joins more than 40 partner countries to mark the 50th anniversary of the @IEA. It’s a chance to prepare for the emerging challenges as we continue to advance energy security and the energy transition for a secure and sustainable future. https://t.co/1ORLtJ5hd8

Members of marginalized and underserved communities are on the frontlines of global challenges – from economic and social inequality to the climate crisis to threats to democracy and peace. We are committed to advancing equity for all. More on our plan: https://t.co/OVAsLdwJzv https://t.co/40yz5CKVp2
Here’s hoping our valentine is a passport to your heart. Sending a big diplomatic pouch of #GovLove to our diplomats, families, and everyone working to create a better world. https://t.co/WurOqzPgYB

For 75+ years, the @FulbrightPrgrm has provided opportunities to pursue study, research, or teach. 

We’re recognizing 170 U.S. institutions that produced the highest number of Fulbright scholars. Did your school make the list? https://t.co/Igs2yO00aF https://t.co/05gKVzMphn

We are working to bring all the hostages home, to ease the humanitarian crisis, and to end the terror threat and to bring peace to Gaza and Israel — an enduring peace with a two-state solution for two peoples. https://t.co/JDBJvKr5Kc

Today @SecBlinken presented the Presidential Award for Extraordinary Efforts to Combat Trafficking in Persons to Dr. Minh Dang @minhspeakstruth and @RestoreNYC. We applaud their significant contributions to combating human trafficking and supporting and partnering with survivors.
Human trafficking is among the worst abuses that exist. We must each play a role in ending it. We cannot turn away. https://t.co/vCdBjf8UpK

The U.S. is preventing and responding to human trafficking. Watch @SecBlinken host the President’s Interagency Task Force to Monitor & Combat Trafficking in Persons, & recognize those who making extraordinary efforts in the fight against human trafficking. https://t.co/FJ5ltSHfIv

.@SecBlinken delivers remarks at the event “Hostage Diplomacy as an International Security Threat: Strengthening our Collective Action, Deterrence and Response” in Washington, D.C. https://t.co/rffrQL3yzZ  https://t.co/6KdasR2w7q

Houthi attacks on ships in the Red Sea and nearby Gulf of Aden have raised costs for businesses. The longer route can add $1 million in fuel costs for a ship bound for northern Europe around the Cape of Good Hope — costs generally passed along to consumers.

Operation Prosperity Guardian, a coalition of more than 20 countries led by the United States, is defending freedom of navigation and securing all nations’ mariners in the Red Sea. We call on all nations to join us in securing stability in this critical maritime region. https://t.co/wpGtRpfP5L

More than one-third of Americans are expected to watch Super Bowl LVIII, including the halftime show starring Usher and all those attention-grabbing advertisements. ShareAmerica breaks down what to watch for in this year’s big game.

This week @SecBlinken continued our intensive diplomacy in the Middle East with his fifth trip to the region since October 7. We are focused on securing the release of all hostages, surging aid to civilians in Gaza, and forging lasting peace and security in the region. https://t.co/xSpxO5X9yr

When more women actively pursue careers in science and technology fields, more is accomplished. The State Department has several initiatives to empower women in STEM fields, including @TechWomen and @TechGirls.

Two years into our Indo-Pacific Strategy, we remain dedicated to working with our partners and allies to realize a shared vision for a region that is free and open, interconnected, prosperous, resilient, and secure. https://t.co/erQB4nyj4v
Today, families are celebrating the Lunar New Year, which this year heralds the Year of the Dragon. Here’s how families in the U.S. are ringing in the year.

Did you know it’s the Year of the Dragon? This weekend in communities across the United States, Asia, and all over the world, people are celebrating #LunarNewYear. It is a time of celebration and welcoming in prosperity and fortune for the coming year. https://t.co/H7sq6tWuYu

The coalition of countries with a common vision of peaceful space cooperation is growing through the #ArtemisAccords. Watch @SecBlinken participate in an Artemis Accords signing ceremony with Greek Foreign Minister Gerapetritis and @NASA’s @SenBillNelson: https://t.co/RRLFkQ6gqY

You can see the path forward for Israel and for the entire region with integration, normalization, security assurances, and the pathway to a Palestinian state. That entirely changes the equation & the future for the better for Israelis, for Arabs, for Palestinians. - @SecBlinken https://t.co/46OZuTfFf9

.@SecBlinken spent the week meeting with officials from across the Middle East to build a more prosperous and secure future for the people of the region. Our @StateDept_NEA Assistant Secretary breaks down the trip. https://t.co/msvQ8jIkEM

Israelis were dehumanized in the most horrific way on October 7th. The hostages have been dehumanized every day since. But that cannot be a license to dehumanize others. The overwhelming majority of people in Gaza had nothing to do with the attacks of October 7th. - @SecBlinken https://t.co/BJ37wdD6Zr

Nearly two million people have been displaced from their homes in Gaza. Hundreds of thousands are experiencing acute hunger. And the daily toll that Israel’s military operations take on innocent civilians remains too high. We must address the ongoing suffering. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/bVPgSDZClc
.@SecBlinken holds a press availability in Tel Aviv, Israel. https://t.co/7vu65WBEKW  https://t.co/9Gr57ESwmc

We will continue to use every tool available to us to reach an extended pause that gets hostages out, that gets more assistance in, that brings calm to Gaza’s civilians, and that keeps diplomacy moving forward toward an integrated and more secure region. -@SecBlinken https://t.co/MW2uFzm2is

Doohyun Kim wants people around the world to look beyond the headlines and hear the story of 25 million North Koreans who are suffering under a brutal regime. He shares why he fled and how the documentary, Beyond Utopia, mirrors his own escape from North Korea. https://t.co/OnuMnldJBz

We’re determined to use any pause to continue to pave a diplomatic path forward to a just and lasting peace and security for the region. That is the best way to ensure that October 7th and the tragic loss of life by Israelis and Palestinians is not repeated. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/CU3bkc0qRI

Iran and its proxies claim that they’re carrying out these attacks somehow on behalf of the Palestinian people. That is absolutely wrong, and it’s a cover for their true intent. — @‌SecBlinken https://t.co/IUOOtzoV6k
.@SecBlinken holds a joint press availability with Qatari Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani in Doha, Qatar. https://t.co/LGFcBmYIbo https://t.co/wmWgNcdfKR
The United States remains focused on working with our partners in the region to secure the release of hostages in Gaza. https://t.co/WeLNbbN2jo

The effects of Houthi attacks in the Red Sea have been devastating for countries in the Middle East. The Houthis claim to act in the interest of the Palestinians in Gaza, but their actions impact the delivery of critical humanitarian aid into Gaza and are harming Palestinians. https://t.co/sJ2dwTyzLO
The U.S. launched Operation Prosperity Guardian with more than 20 countries to defend the freedom of navigation and provide increased security in Red Sea shipping lanes. https://t.co/MPZKfVV8yz

The Houthis’ reckless actions are impacting the delivery of essential goods, like grain and medical supplies, and lifesaving aid to people who need it most, such as those in Yemen and Gaza. https://t.co/2CuYMDM1zR

Hundreds of ships carrying vital supplies now need to go 4,000 miles around the southern tip of Africa to avoid the Red Sea. This rerouting adds about 10 days of travel and significant costs. https://t.co/MUnmUwSPCU
Starting late last year, Yemen’s Iran-backed Houthis launched unprecedented attacks against ships from over 40 countries in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden. https://t.co/IS5uHE9N40

You may not realize that Houthi attacks against commercial shipping vessels in the Red Sea are directly impacting people’s lives. Ships taking longer journeys increase the cost of food, medicine, and fuel, as well as life-saving aid to people who need it most. ⤵️ https://t.co/ETnUT5NrFB
The attacks by the Houthis are hurting people around the world – especially the poorest and most vulnerable populations, including in Yemen and Gaza. https://t.co/RfElWsL0p7

Wetlands help protect us from storms, purify drinking water, host endangered species, and are crucial for the fishing industry, yet are disappearing at an alarming rate. We are working with partners to conserve and sustainably manage the world's wetlands and ecosystems. https://t.co/6tQSAFOcie

This #BlackHistoryMonth, we’re highlighting the inaugural members of @POTUS's Advisory Council on African Diaspora Engagement. These leaders strengthen our U.S.-Africa partnership. https://t.co/5vw6ctJ2uC

To mark the start of #BlackHistoryMonth, learn a bit about some of our country’s historic African American diplomats. Their legacy continues to inspire generations of diplomats. https://t.co/DFYcq1cRke

.@POTUS and @SecBlinken have been clear that extremist violence in the West Bank is unacceptable. Under the President's new Executive Order, the State Department is imposing financial sanctions on four Israeli nationals for their destabilizing acts. https://t.co/rXNvknBKH5

The United States calls on the military regime to end its violence against the people of Burma, to release those unjustly and arbitrarily detained, to allow unhindered humanitarian access, and respect the will of the people for return to the path towards representative democracy. https://t.co/d5kazDYIYP
